title: chrysanthemum corymbosum subsp. clusii
intro: Chrysanthemum corymbosum subsp. clusii, also known as the Gold Thread or Hardy Chrysanthemum, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to China and is cultivated for its ornamental and medicinal value. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, headaches, and respiratory issues. The Gold Thread has been shown to have various health benefits, including reducing stress, improving sleep quality, and boosting the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Chrysanthemum corymbosum subsp. clusii typically include infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or used topically to promote relaxation and soothe skin irritations.
